data.title: OpenAI Kills Sora Overnight, Gives No Warning to Disney (DIS)
data.description:
OpenAI, the artificial intelligence company co-founded by Elon Musk, has abruptly stopped its AI video tool, Sora, ending a planned collaboration with Walt Disney (DIS). This announcement was made 30 minutes after both companies met to discuss the collaboration. The decision shows that OpenAI is shifting towards more commercially focused areas with better monetization. 

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sudden Shutdown Ends Ongoing Disney Collaboration 

Teams from Walt Disney and OpenAI were actively working on a project linked to Sora and had met shortly before the decision to shut down the tool was publicly announced. According to the people familiar with the matter, the startled Disney team learnt about the abrupt halt in development soon after the meeting, leaving ample time between the ongoing collaboration and the announcement. 

This decision has ended a three-year agreement between the two companies, following Disney's plan to invest $1 billion in the Sora project. A major part of the deal was that Disney was expected to invest in OpenAI and provide access to more than 200 of its characters for AI-generated video content. However, the transaction has not been completed, and there was no exchange of funds

OpenAI's Shift Towards Enterprise and Resource Constraints

OpenAI has decided to discontinue Sora because it is now focusing its strategy on areas that can generate stronger commercial returns, such as enterprise services and coding tools. This move shows the company is prioritizing business segments with clearer monetization potential as the AI ecosystem is becoming more competitive. 

According to reliable sources, operating the AI video tool required substantial computational resources, which strained other teams within the company. The reallocation of resources suggests a shift towards optimizing infrastructure and promoting products with higher demand from corporate clients.

Is Disney Stock a Good Investment in 2026?

Investors evaluating Disney (DIS) often judge its performance based on streaming, theme parks, and overall earnings when considering the stock's outlook. TipRanks Forecast data from ratings of Wall Street analysts reveal that the DIS stock is a "Strong Buy" right now. The stock has zero sell ratings and has been projected to increase to $133.9, on average. Just like it did with the private company, OpenAI, Disney has been exploring partnerships with firms in the AI sector, aiming to boost its performance.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: 7% Dividend Yield and Double-Digit Upside: Truist Suggests 2 Dividend Stocks to Buy
data.description:
Nearly three months into 2026, with the geopolitical situation showing increasing risk and the American political situation showing increased deadlocks on Capitol Hill, it's only natural for investors to look for ways to shore up their portfolios. One classic play – buy into high-yielding dividend stocks.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Dividend stocks have plenty to offer investors right now – and always. The best of them combine high yields with reliable payment histories, giving investors a steady income stream that's an asset whether the markets run up or down.

Wall Street's analysts know this, and the team at Truist has been tagging some high-yield dividend payers for investor attention. Of particular interest, two of these picks also feature double-digit upside potential for the coming year. Add in the dividend yields that start at 7%, and we're looking at solid returns that will be a boon for any investing strategy.

To get the full picture, we turned to the TipRanks database to see how these Truist picks stack up with the rest of the Street. Let's take a closer look.

Diversified Energy Company (DEC)

The first high-yielding dividend stock we'll look at is Diversified Energy Company, a producer, transporter, and marketer of natural gas and natural gas liquids in the Appalachian and Central regions of the US. Diversified follows a coherent strategy of acquiring existing production wells that feature long-life and low-decline profiles. The company operates these wells, along with midstream assets, through an efficient management style that aims to improve or restore asset production while reducing the cost of operations. The company also works to safely retire wells that reach the end of their productive lives.

Diversified's upstream production leans heavily toward Appalachia, where the company has 70% of its producing assets. The remaining 30% of production comes from the Central region. These production assets are backed up by a midstream network that includes over 17,000 miles of gathering and transportation pipelines. Integrating these sets of assets – upstream and midstream – allows Diversified to create stable operating margins and reliable cash flows.

On the gas marketing side, Diversified acts in 25 markets and has 261 actively managed sales points. This network handles more than 1.2 billion bcf of natural gas every day, and puts the company among the top 25 of North American natural gas marketers.

Like many energy sector operators, Diversified pays out a regular quarterly dividend. The company last declared the payment on February 26 for a June 30 payout; the dividend was set at 29 cents per common share. At that rate, the dividend annualizes to $1.16 and gives a forward yield of 7%.

On the financial side, Diversified generated $667 million in revenue during its last reported quarter, 4Q25. For the full-year 2025, revenue came to $1.83 billion, beating the forecast by $190 million and registering an impressive 141% year-over-year increase. The company had a full-year net income of $342 million, and a 2025 adjusted free cash flow of $440 million.

Truist analyst Gabe Daoud lays out an upbeat outlook for Diversified. He says of the company, "DEC stands out as a differentiated small-cap E&P owing to the company's PDP (proved developed producing) roll-up strategy. DEC securitizes low-decline, stable, and heavily hedged (80%+) PDP cash flowing assets providing stable cash flow to support an attractive dividend yield of ~8%, one of the highest in our coverage list… Valuation stands at 3.7x '26e EV/EBITDA – roughly 1 turns below gas -focused E&P peers – and FCF/EV yield at 30.6%. We believe the current valuation/multiple construct of DEC could re -rate higher closer to peers due to its PDP roll-up strategy."

Quantifying his stance here, Daoud puts a Buy rating on DEC, and he complements that with a $22 price target that indicates a one-year upside potential of 32%. The forward dividend yield brings the one-year return here to 39%.

Overall, the Street gives this stock a unanimously positive Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 6 recent positive analyst reviews. The shares are trading for $16.62 and have an average target price of $21.71; this combination gives a 31% upside potential on the one-year horizon. (See DEC stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: UPS Opens New $100M Logistics Hub in Taiwan to Meet Rampant TSMC Chip Demand
data.description:
United Parcel Services (UPS) has opened a new $100 million logistics centre in Taiwan to match surging demand for semiconductor giant TSMC (TSM).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

New Asian Distribution Center

UPS said the site in Northern Taiwan's Taoyuan will be its largest in the Asia Pacific and will be used by both TSMC and U.S. semiconductor maker Applied Materials (AMAT) as an Asian distribution center.

"Around 80% of the freight is high-tech," said Lauren ​Zhao, president ​of UPS Asia Pacific ​Supply Chain Solutions and ‌Freight Forwarding. "Everyone knows that Taiwan's semiconductor industry is the most advanced in the world, and the manufacturing processes related to the semiconductor industry are also where Taiwan is ‌leading the world."

TSMC is ramping up its production in Taiwan. It is building a large new factory in Kaohsiung as part of a move to develop a semiconductor cluster in Southern Taiwan. According to reports TSMC plans to build several new 2-nanometer process fabs in Hsinchu and Kaohsiung.

TSMC Investing in Taiwan

Overall, the Taiwan company plans to spend between $52 billion to $56 billion this year, up 27 to 37% from last year as demand continues to rise.

According to TSMC, 60 to 80% of this year's spend will go towards advanced process development, 10% to specialty processes, and 10 to 20% to high-end IC (integrated circuit) packaging, testing, photomasking and other items.

The UPS move in Taiwan is a clever one given concerns over a possible global economic slowdown as a result of the Iran war. Demand for AI and chips should stay elevated even during tough times, as well as a need for manufacturers and customers to find new sources of distribution and supply.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: VCX Fund Soars 1,000% in 5 Days as SpaceX IPO Hype Nears Fever Pitch — DXYZ on the Rise
data.description:
Shares in the Fundrise Innovation Fund (VCX) climbed over $340 per share early Wednesday, marking about a 991% jump from its opening price of $31.25 five days ago. The closed-end fund has continued to surge as more retail investors chase pre-IPO (initial public offering) exposure to heavyweight private companies on Wall Street, with recent headlines from Elon Musk's SpaceX likely adding more fuel to the fire.

According to reports, the space exploration company could file its IPO prospectus as early as this week or the next, with SpaceX expected to raise more than $75 billion from its debut at a valuation of $1.25 trillion. In addition to this, Musk has recently stated plans to pump about $25 billion into building a new chip-manufacturing facility for Tesla (TSLA), SpaceX, and xAI.

Apart from VCX, the Destiny Tech 100 (DXYZ), which was launched in 2022 and debuted on the New York Stock Exchange in 2024, is now also attracting attention. DXYZ was up about 18% to $31.39 per share as of 6:34 a.m. EST, adding to its 9.27% gain from the previous day.

data.title: China Cracks Down on Food Delivery Price Wars, Lifting Alibaba (BABA), JD.com (JD), and Meituan Stocks
data.description:
China tech stocks moved higher after regulators signaled a shift in how food delivery firms compete, and that could be a quiet win for Alibaba Group Holding (BABA). Shares of Meituan (MPNGF) jumped as much as 13% in Hong Kong, while Alibaba gained about 6.7% and JD.com (JD) rose 5.7%. The move came after China's market regulator held a meeting to curb unfair competition across the sector.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, a state-backed Economic Daily column called for an end to the ongoing price wars. The report said the industry has fallen into a "vicious cycle" of losing money just to gain attention, which in turn weighs on consumer recovery.

Meanwhile, BABA shares dropped 0.46% on the NYSE on Tuesday, closing at $125.48.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: SpaceX vs. Anthropic: VCX and DXYZ Funds Offer Indirect Exposure to Private AI/Space Tech
data.description:
In the competitive world of private tech, SpaceX and Anthropic lead their fields. SpaceX dominates space travel with reusable rockets, Starlink's global internet constellation, and frequent NASA contracts. Anthropic advances AI safety through models like Claude, backed by heavyweights such as Amazon (AMZN) and Google (GOOGL).

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade AMZN with leverage
  • New trading tool for AMZN bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade AMZN

Direct investment in these private companies remains unavailable to most retail investors due to their non-public status. However, two publicly traded funds: the Fundrise Innovation Fund, LLC (VCX) and Destiny Tech100 Inc. (DXYZ) offer indirect access to these unicorns. Below, we examine their holdings, performance, and potential.

VCX's Rapid Rise Driven By Anthropic Exposure

Fundrise Innovation Fund is a closed-end interval fund, which has surged nearly 900% since its recent listing. The fund trades at a significant premium to its net asset value (NAV) of $18.26 per share as of early 2026, as per Fundrise disclosures. This premium reflects strong investor demand, though it introduces volatility as hype fades.

VCX's portfolio boasts a hefty 20.7% allocation to Anthropic, a standout amid the AI boom. The fund focuses on late-stage ventures in transformative tech, with quarterly redemption windows providing some liquidity.

DXYZ: A Diversified Bet on SpaceX and Anthropic

Destiny Tech100 (DXYZ), another closed-end fund, holds substantial SpaceX stakes and added $100 million in Anthropic exposure via a special purpose vehicle (SPV) on February 11, 2026. This brings its combined weighting in SpaceX and Anthropic to around 20%, mirroring VCX's emphasis.

DXYZ once traded far above NAV but now hovers just dollars over it, with 17% short interest suggesting rebound potential. It offers stock-like liquidity for otherwise illiquid private assets. DXYZ currently holds 27 companies, but targets 100 in total, spanning AI, space, biotech, and more.

Why a Dual SpaceX + Anthropic Bet Is Better

SpaceX, valued at around $350 billion, boasts tangible milestones: over 100 launches last year and multi-billion-dollar government deals. Anthropic, at around $61.5 billion, competes fiercely with OpenAI in safe AI development.

Funds like VCX and DXYZ democratize access to these "locked-up" assets. DXYZ's broader Tech100 basket adds diversification, while VCX's Anthropic focus has fueled its gains, potentially lifting DXYZ via shared sentiment.

Investing in these funds also poses risks such as premium/discount volatility, trading well above or below NAV, alongside illiquidity from private holdings that limit quick exits. VCX, for instance, imposes quarterly redemption caps of 5-25%. Tech valuations also swing wildly (DXYZ plunged over 80% in 2025), compounded by high management fees of 2-3%. Even so, for fans of AI/space, these funds are a smart proxy showdown.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: New Netgear Trade Idea After FCC Ban Drives a Boost
data.description:
It happened overnight. The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) announced a ban on the sale of new foreign-made consumer router models. 

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

More than 60% of routers sold in the U.S. are estimated to have been manufactured in mainland China. According to the federal agency, cyberattacks on small businesses and households in the U.S. have been on a rapid rise since 2024. The FCC feels that this is at least partially due to national security concerns posed by foreign-made routers. Routers that are already here and have already been approved are still eligible for sale in the U.S. This applies to new routers.

Netgear (NTGR) is an interesting case. The firm is headquartered in the U.S., and its equipment is designed in the U.S. However, that gear is manufactured elsewhere and would be included in the ban. Netgear, some expect, will be granted a conditional approval as a U.S. company by either the Department of Defense/War or Department of Homeland Security. NTGR stock is up more than 10% in the past day.

Could Use the Help

Netgear could use the help. The firm posted adjusted earnings and revenue beats for its fiscal fourth quarter. Those results were released in early February. There was no sales growth, however. For the current quarter, which is due to be reported in late April, the few analysts that actually cover this name are looking for an adjusted EPS of -$0.08 on revenue of $158.7 million. That number would be good for a year-over-year contraction of roughly 2%.

Interestingly, on this news, analyst Tore Svanberg of Stifel Nicolaus, who is rated at five stars (out of five) by TipRanks, opined on the stock. Svanberg reiterated both his "Buy" rating and his $36 target price. The stock is not heavily followed on Wall Street.

The Chart​
data.author: TheStreet Pro

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/25/26 – Stock Futures Rise on U.S.-Iran Peace Negotiations
data.description:
U.S. stock futures climbed early Wednesday after President Trump announced that the U.S. had sent a 15-point peace plan to Iran. Oil prices fell sharply due to rising hopes for de-escalation. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 1.05%, 0.89%, and 0.93%, respectively, at 5:27 a.m. EST on March 25.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was down 1.01% at $98.92 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 1.05% to $87.90 per barrel.

During Tuesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower, reversing earlier gains from President Trump's claims of "productive conversations." Markets dipped after Iranian officials denied direct peace talks with the U.S. The Dow fell 0.18%, the Nasdaq declined 0.84%, and the S&P 500 shed 0.37%.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield fell to near 4.33%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price hovered around $4,565 per ounce on Wednesday.

No key economic reports are scheduled today. On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Beyond Meat (BYND), Chewy (CHWY), PDD Holdings (PDD), Paychex (PAYX), and Jefferies (JEF).

Elsewhere, European indexes opened higher today amid signs of U.S. efforts to de-escalate tensions.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets advanced on March 25, as global investors reacted to easing U.S.-Iran tensions.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 1.09%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 1.30%, while the Shenzhen Component surged 1.99%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei increased 2.87% and the Topix added 2.57%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Meta Platforms Boosts Top Executives' Pay with Stock Options amid AI Push
data.description:
Social media giant Meta Platforms (META) is offering its top executives stock options in an effort to retain talent as the AI race heats up. This is the first time that the company has offered its top executives stock options since its initial public offering (IPO) in 2012. META stock was up 1.1% in Wednesday's pre-market trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Offers Stock Options to Incentivize Key Talent

Meta Platforms' decision to offer stock options comes as the company is trying to gain an edge in generative AI and compete more effectively by retaining key AI talent.

According to SEC (Securities and Exchange Commission) filings, CFO Susan Li, chief technology officer Andrew Bosworth, chief product officer Chris Cox, COO Javier Olivan, president Dina Powell McCormick, and chief legal officer Curtis Mahoney ​are eligible for the stock options. Interestingly, CEO Mark Zuckerberg is not getting any options.

In a statement, a Meta spokesperson called the move a "big bet." He added that these "pay packages will not be realized unless Meta achieves massive future success," and the share price significantly exceeds the exercise price. The first tranche of options will be paid when META stock hits $1,116.08, an 88% jump from Tuesday's closing price of $592.92. The next tranche requires a stock price of $1,393.87, while the highest tranche targets a price of $3,727.12.

With OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google-parent Alphabet (GOOGL) aggressively launching AI models and tools, Meta Platforms is trying to remain competitive in the AI race. The company announced a capex in the range of $115 billion to $135 billion for 2026 to support its AI push. Meta's capital spending forecast for 2026 marks a significant jump from the $72.2 billion spent last year.

Is META a Good Stock to Buy Now?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on Meta Platforms stock based on 40 Buys and five Holds. The average META stock price target of $865.58 indicates 46% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
